{\n461\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n462\t            }\n463\t            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n464\t parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n468\t            }\n469\t\n470\t            if (parts.length > 0) {\n471\t                el.innerHTML = '📊   }\n477\t\n478\t        async function registerSurface() {\n479\t            try {\n480\t                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n481\t                    method: {\n495\t                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n496\t                throw error;\n497\t            }\n498\t        }\n499\t\n500\t        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n501\t        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n502\t        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n503\t        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n504\t        let welcomeProjects = null;\n505\t\n506\t        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n507\t            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n508\t            try {\n509\t                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n510\t                if (resp.ok) {\n511\t                    const data = await resp.json();\n512\t                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n513\t                    return welcomeProjects;\n514\t                }\n515\t            } catch (error) {\n516\t                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n517\t            }\n518\t            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n519\t            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n520\t            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n521\t            welcomeProjects = [];\n522\t            return welcomeProjects;\n523\t        }\n524\t\n525\t        async function loadTopics() {\n526\t            try {\n527\t                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n528\t                if (!response.ok) return;\n529\t\n530\t                const data = await response.json();\n531\t                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n532\t                const cards = data.cards || [];\n533\t\n534\t                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n535\t                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n536\t                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n537\t                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n538\t                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n539\t                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n540\t                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n541\t                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n542\t                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n543\t\n544\t                container.innerHTML = '';\n545\t                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n546\t                    container.appendChild(node);\n547\t                });\n548\t            } catch (error) {\n549\t                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n550\t            }\n551\t        }\n552\t\n553\t        function connectSSE() {\n554\t            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n555\t\n556\t            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n557\t            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n558\t\n559\t            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n560\t                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n561\t                loadTopics();\n562\t            };\n563\t\n564\t            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n565\t                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n566\t                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n567\t            };\n568\t\n569\t            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n570\t                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n571\t                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n572\t            });\n573\t\n574\t            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n575\t                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n576\t                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n577\t            });\n578\t\n579\t            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n580\t                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n581\t                console.log('New result:', result);\n582\t                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n583\t            });\n584\t\n585\t            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n586\t                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n587\t                console.log('Topic updated:',   font-size: 20px;\n            font-weight: 500;\n            margin-bottom: 8px;\n            color: #94a3b8;\n        }\n\n        .empty-state p {\n            font-size: 14px;\n        }\n\n        /* Loading state */\n        .loading {\n            display: flex;\n            justify-content: center;\n            align-items: center;\n            padding: 40px;\n            color: #64748b;\n          animation: spin 1s linear infinite;\n            margin-right: 12px;\n        }\n\n        @keyframes spin {\n            to { transform: rotate(360deg); }\n        }\n    </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n    <div class=\"container\">\n        <header>\n            <div style=\"display:flex;align-items:baseline;gap:8px\">\n                <h1>ADC Canvas</h1>\n <span class=\"status-dot\" id=\"statusDot\"></span>\n                <span id=\"statusText\">Connecting...</span>\n            </div>\n        </header>\n\n        <div class=\"workload-summary\" placeholder=\"Ask anything — status of the options pipeline, what's in flight, open beads…\"\n                rows=\"1\"\n            ></textarea>\n            <button class=\"btn-mic\" id=\"btnMic\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <path d=\"M12 2a3 3 0 0 0-3 3v7a3 3 0 0 0 6 0V5a3 3 0 0 0-3-3z\"/>\n                    <path d=\"M19 10v2a7 7 0 0 1-14 0v-2\"/>\n                    <line x1=\"12\" y1=\"19\" x2=\"12\" y2=\"23\"/>\n                    <line x1=\"8\" y1=\"23\" x2=\"16\" y2=\"23\"/>\n                </svg>\n            </button>\n            <button class=\"btn-send\" id=\"btnSend\" title=\"Send\" disabled>\n                <svg width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\"      </svg>\n            </button>\n        </div>\n\n        <div id=\"topicsContainer\" class=\"topics-grid\">\n            <div class=\"loading\">\n                <div class=\"spinner\"></div>\n                <span>Loading topics...</span>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <!-- Render helpers (createTopicCard + friends) — loaded before the app\n         script so they're available as globals and unit-testable headlessly. -->\n    <script src=\"/canvas.js\"></script>\n    <script>\n        // Session and surface IDs from URL params or generate new\n        const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);\n        const sessionId = urlParams.get('session_id') || generateId();\n        const surfaceType = 'canvas';\n\n        let surfaceId = null;\n        let eventSource = null;\n\n        // Update URL with session_id for reload persistence\n        if (!urlParams.get('session_id')) {\n            urlParams.set('session_id', sessionId);\n            window.history.replaceState({}, '', `${window.location.pathname}?${urlParams}`);\n        }\n\n        function generateId() {\n            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n        }\n\n        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel live\n        // in /canvas.js (loaded below as a blocking <script src> before this\n        // block), so they are available as globals here and unit-testable\n        // headlessly. See src/canvas/canvas.js.\n\n        function updateConnectionStatus(status, text) {\n            const dot = document.getElementById('statusDot');\n            const statusText = document.getElementById('statusText');\n\n            dot.className = 'status-dot ' + status;\n            statusText.textContent = text;\n        }\n\n        function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n            const parts = [];\n\n            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n            }\n            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n            }\n            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n            }\n\n            if (parts.length > 0) {\n                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n                el.classList.add('show');\n            } else {\n                el.classList.remove('show');\n            }\n        }\n\n        async function registerSurface() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        session_id: sessionId,\n                        surface_type: surfaceType\n                    })\n                });\n\n                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n                return surfaceId;\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n                throw error;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n        let welcomeProjects = null;\n\n        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n                if (resp.ok) {\n                    const data = await resp.json();\n                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n                    return welcomeProjects;\n                }\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n            }\n            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n            welcomeProjects = [];\n            return welcomeProjects;\n        }\n\n        async function loadTopics() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n                if (!response.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n                const cards = data.cards || [];\n\n                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n                    container.appendChild(node);\n                });\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        function connectSSE() {\n            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n\n            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n\n            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n                loadTopics();\n            };\n\n            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n            };\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('New result:', result);\n                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n                loadTopics();\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Server disconnect:', data);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n                eventSource.close();\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Heartbeat to keep surface alive\n        setInterval(() => {\n            if (surfaceId) {\n                fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        surface_id: surfaceId,\n                        session_id: sessionId\n                    })\n                }).catch(console.error);\n            }\n        }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds\n\n        // ── Input bar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        const textarea = document.getElementById('inputTextarea');\n        const btnSend  = document.getElementById('btnSend');\n        const btnMic   = document.getElementById('btnMic');\n\n        // Auto-resize textarea\n        textarea.addEventListener('input', () => {\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n            btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n        });\n\n        // Send on Enter (Shift+Enter = newline)\n        textarea.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {\n                e.preventDefault();\n                if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n            }\n        });\n\n        btnSend.addEventListener('click', () => {\n            if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n        });\n\n        async function dispatch(utterance) {\n            if (!utterance) return;\n            textarea.value = '';\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            btnSend.disabled = true;\n\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/dispatch', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId }),\n                });\n                if (!resp.ok) {\n                    const err = await resp.text();\n                    console.error('Dispatch error:', err);\n                }\n            } catch (err) {\n                console.error('Dispatch failed:', err);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Web Speech API mic\n        let recognition = null;\n\n        if ('webkitSpeechRecognition' in window || 'SpeechRecognition' in window) {\n            const SpeechRecognition = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition;\n            recognition = new SpeechRecognition();\n            recognition.continuous = false;\n            recognition.interimResults = true;\n            recognition.lang = 'en-US';\n\n            let finalTranscript = '';\n\n            recognition.onstart = () => {\n                btnMic.classList.add('recording');\n                finalTranscript = textarea.value;\n            };\n\n            recognition.onresult = (e) => {\n                let interim = '';\n                for (let i = e.resultIndex; i < e.results.length; i++) {\n                    if (e.results[i].isFinal) {\n                        finalTranscript += e.results[i][0].transcript;\n                    } else {\n                        interim += e.results[i][0].transcript;\n                    }\n                }\n                textarea.value = finalTranscript + interim;\n                textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n                textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n                btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n            };\n\n            recognition.onend = () => {\n                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n                if (finalTranscript.trim()) dispatch(finalTranscript.trim());\n            };\n\n            recognition.onerror = (e) => {\n                console.error('Speech recognition error:', e.error);\n                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n            };\n\n            btnMic.addEventListener('click', () => {\n                if (btnMic.classList.contains('recording')) {\n                    recognition.stop();\n                } else {\n                    recognition.start();\n                }\n            });\n        } else {\n            // Fallback: MediaRecorder + whisper-stt for browsers without Web Speech API\n            let mediaRecorder = null;\n            let audioChunks = [];\n\n            async function startRecording() {\n                try {\n                    const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true });\n                    mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(stream);\n                    audioChunks = [];\n\n                    mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (event) => {\n                        if (event.data.size > 0) {\n                            audioChunks.push(event.data);\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    mediaRecorder.onstop = async () => {\n                        btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n                        btnMic.innerHTML = '🎤';\n\n                        // Create audio blob and convert to base64\n                        const audioBlob = new Blob(audioChunks, { type: 'audio/webm' });\n                        const reader = new FileReader();\n                        reader.onloadend = async () => {\n                            const base64Audio = reader.result.split(',')[1];\n\n                            try {\n                                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/stt', {\n                                    method: 'POST',\n                                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                                        audio_data: base64Audio,\n                                        format: 'webm'\n                                    })\n                                });\n\n                                if (resp.ok) {\n                                    const data = await resp.json();\n                                    if (data.text) {\n                                        textarea.value = data.text;\n                                        textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n                                        textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n                                        btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n                                        // Auto-dispatch on successful transcription\n                                        dispatch(data.text.trim());\n                                    }\n                                } else {\n                                    const err = await resp.json();\n                                    console.error('STT error:', err);\n                                    btnMic.title = `Transcription failed: ${err.error || 'Unknown error'}`;\n                                    setTimeout(() => {\n                                        btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n                                    }, 3000);\n                                }\n                            } catch (err) {\n                                console.error('STT request failed:', err);\n                                btnMic.title = 'STT service unavailable';\n                                setTimeout(() => {\n                                    btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n                                }, 3000);\n                            }\n                        };\n                        reader.readAsDataURL(audioBlob);\n\n                        // Stop all tracks to release microphone\n                        stream.getTracks().forEach(track => track.stop());\n                    };\n\n                    mediaRecorder.start();\n                    btnMic.classList.add('recording');\n                    btnMic.innerHTML = '⏹';\n                } catch (err) {\n                    console.error('MediaRecorder access failed:', err);\n                    btnMic.title = 'Microphone access denied';\n                    btnMic.style.opacity = '0.4';\n                    btnMic.disabled = true;\n                }\n            }\n\n            function stopRecording() {\n                if (mediaRecorder && mediaRecorder.state === 'recording') {\n                    mediaRecorder.stop();\n                }\n            }\n\n            btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n            btnMic.disabled = false;\n            btnMic.style.opacity = '1';\n\n            btnMic.addEventListener('click', () => {\n                if (btnMic.classList.contains('recording')) {\n                    stopRecording();\n                } else {\n                    startRecording();\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        // ── Initialize ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        // Initialize\n        async function init() {\n            try {\n                // Show version\n                const meta = await fetch('/openapi.json').then(r => r.json()).catch(() => null);\n                if (meta && meta.info && meta.info.version) {\n                    document.getElementById('versionBadge').textContent = 'v' + meta.info.version;\n                }\n\n                await registerSurface();\n                connectSSE();\n            } catch (error) {\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Failed to connect');\n                console.error('Initialization error:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        init();\n    </script>\n\n    <!-- ── Agentation: element-selector feedback toolbar ────────────────────── -->\n    <script>\n    (function () {\n        var active = false;\n        var selectedEl = null;\n\n        // Build a stable CSS selector for a given element\n        function buildSelector(el) {\n            if (!el || el === document.body) return 'body';\n            var parts = [];\n            var cur = el;\n            while (cur && cur !== document.body && cur !== document.documentElement) {\n                if (cur.id) { parts.unshift('#' + cur.id); break; }\n                var tag = cur.tagName.toLowerCase();\n                var classes = Array.from(cur.classList)\n                    .filter(function (c) { return c.length > 1; })\n                    .slice(0, 2);\n                var part = tag + (classes.length ? '.' + classes.join('.') : '');\n                // add nth-of-type when siblings share the same tag\n                if (cur.parentElement) {\n                    var sibs = Array.from(cur.parentElement.children)\n                        .filter(function (s) { return s.tagName === cur.tagName; });\n                    if (sibs.length > 1) part += ':nth-of-type(' + (sibs.indexOf(cur) + 1) + ')';\n                }\n                parts.unshift(part);\n                cur = cur.parentElement;\n            }\n            return parts.join(' > ');\n        }\n\n        // Highlight box\n        var hlBox = document.createElement('div');\n        hlBox.style.cssText = [\n            'position:fixed', 'pointer-events:none', 'z-index:9998',\n            'border:2px solid #3b82f6', 'background:rgba(59,130,246,.08)',\n            'border-radius:4px', 'display:none', 'transition:all .08s'\n        ].join(';');\n        document.body.appendChild(hlBox);\n\n        function moveHighlight(el) {\n            if (!el || el === document.body) { hlBox.style.display = 'none'; return; }\n            var r = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n            hlBox.style.cssText = hlBox.style.cssText; // keep existing\n            hlBox.style.display = 'block';\n            hlBox.style.left  = (r.left   - 2) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.top   = (r.top    - 2) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.width = (r.width  + 4) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.height= (r.height + 4) + 'px';\n        }\n\n        // Popover\n        var pop = document.createElement('div');\n        pop.style.cssText = [\n            'position:fixed', 'z-index:9999', 'display:none', 'width:300px',\n            'background:#1e293b', 'border:1px solid #3b82f6', 'border-radius:8px',\n            'padding:12px', 'box-shadow:0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,.5)',\n            'font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,\"Segoe UI\",Roboto,sans-serif'\n        ].join(';');\n        pop.innerHTML = [\n            '<div id=\"atn-sel\" style=\"font-size:11px;color:#64748b;margin-bottom:6px;',\n                'font-family:monospace;overflow:hidden;text-overflow:ellipsis;white-space:nowrap\"></div>',\n            '<textarea id=\"atn-txt\" rows=\"3\" placeholder=\"Describe what to change…\" style=\"',\n                'width:100%;box-sizing:border-box;background:#0f1419;border:1px solid #334155;',\n                'color:#e2e8f0;border-radius:6px;padding:8px;font-size:13px;',\n                'resize:none;font-family:inherit;outline:none\"></textarea>',\n            '<div style=\"display:flex;gap:6px;margin-top:8px\">',\n                '<button id=\"atn-cancel\" style=\"flex:1;padding:6px;background:#334155;border:none;',\n                    'color:#94a3b8;border-radius:6px;cursor:pointer;font-size:13px\">Cancel</button>',\n                '<button id=\"atn-send\" style=\"flex:1;padding:6px;background:#3b82f6;border:none;',\n                    'color:#fff;border-radius:6px;cursor:pointer;font-size:13px;font-weight:500\">',\n                    'Send feedback</button>',\n            '</div>'\n        ].join('');\n        document.body.appendChild(pop);\n\n        function positionPop(el) {\n            var r = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n            var top  = r.bottom + 8;\n            var left = r.left;\n            if (top  + 180 > window.innerHeight) top  = r.top - 188;\n            if (left + 308 > window.innerWidth)  left = window.innerWidth - 316;\n            if (left < 8) left = 8;\n            pop.style.top  = top  + 'px';\n            pop.style.left = left + 'px';\n            pop.style.display = 'block';\n        }\n\n        // Toggle button (bottom-right corner)\n        var toggleBtn = document.createElement('button');\n        toggleBtn.id = 'atn-btn';\n        toggleBtn.textContent = '✏️ Feedback';\n        toggleBtn.title = 'Click any element to give UI feedback';\n        toggleBtn.style.cssText = [\n            'position:fixed', 'bottom:16px', 'right:16px', 'z-index:9997',\n            'padding:8px 14px', 'background:#1e293b', 'border:1px solid #334155',\n            'color:#94a3b8', 'border-radius:20px', 'cursor:pointer', 'font-size:13px',\n            'font-family:inherit', 'transition:all .15s'\n        ].join(';');\n        document.body.appendChild(toggleBtn);\n\n        function enterMode() {\n            active = true;\n            toggleBtn.style.background = '#1e3a5f';\n            toggleBtn.style.borderColor = '#3b82f6';\n            toggleBtn.style.color = '#60a5fa';\n            toggleBtn.textContent = '✕ Cancel';\n            document.body.style.cursor = 'crosshair';\n        }\n\n        function exitMode() {\n            active = false;\n            selectedEl = null;\n            hlBox.style.display = 'none';\n            pop.style.display = 'none';\n            toggleBtn.style.background = '#1e293b';\n            toggleBtn.style.borderColor = '#334155';\n            toggleBtn.style.color = '#94a3b8';\n            toggleBtn.textContent = '✏️ Feedback';\n            document.body.style.cursor = '';\n            document.getElementById('atn-txt').value = '';\n        }\n\n        toggleBtn.addEventListener('click', function () {\n            if (active) exitMode(); else enterMode();\n        });\n\n        document.addEventListener('mousemove', function (e) {\n            if (!active || pop.style.display === 'block') return;\n            var t = e.target;\n            if (t === toggleBtn || t === hlBox || t.closest('#atn-popover')) return;\n            moveHighlight(t);\n        });\n\n        document.addEventListener('click', function (e) {\n            if (!active) return;\n            if (e.target === toggleBtn || e.target.closest && e.target.closest('[id^=\"atn-\"]')) return;\n            e.preventDefault();\n            e.stopPropagation();\n            selectedEl = e.target;\n            document.getElementById('atn-sel').textContent = buildSelector(selectedEl);\n            document.getElementById('atn-txt').value = '';\n            positionPop(selectedEl);\n            setTimeout(function () { document.getElementById('atn-txt').focus(); }, 30);\n        }, true);\n\n        document.getElementById('atn-cancel').addEventListener('click', exitMode);\n\n        document.getElementById('atn-txt').addEventListener('keydown', function (e) {\n            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) { e.preventDefault(); sendFeedback(); }\n            if (e.key === 'Escape') exitMode();\n        });\n\n        document.getElementById('atn-send').addEventListener('click', sendFeedback);\n\n        async function sendFeedback() {\n            var text = document.getElementById('atn-txt').value.trim();\n            if (!text || !selectedEl) return;\n            var sel     = buildSelector(selectedEl);\n            var elHtml  = selectedEl.outerHTML.slice(0, 2000);\n            var sendBtn = document.getElementById('atn-send');\n            sendBtn.textContent = 'Sending…';\n            sendBtn.disabled = true;\n            try {\n                await fetch('/api/v1/feedback', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        feedback: text,\n                        feedback_type: 'component_iteration',\n                        context: { selector: sel, element_html: elHtml },\n                        session_id: sessionId,\n                    }),\n                });\n                showToast('Feedback sent');\n            } catch (err) {\n                showToast('Failed to send', true);\n                console.error('Feedback error:', err);\n            }\n            exitMode();\n        }\n\n        function showToast(msg, isError) {\n            var t = document.createElement('div');\n            t.style.cssText = [\n                'position:fixed', 'bottom:56px', 'right:16px', 'z-index:10000',\n                'background:' + (isError ? '#7f1d1d' : '#166534'),\n                'color:'      + (isError ? {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_6576d934e415484ba248df4b","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\t * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n3\t *\n4\t * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n5\t * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n6\t * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n7\t * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n8\t * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n9\t * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n10\t * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n11\t * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n12\t * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n13\t * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n14\t * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n15\t *\n16\t * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n17\t * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n18\t * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n19\t * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n20\t * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n21\t * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n22\t * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n23\t * escaping contract.\n24\t *\n25\t * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n26\t * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n27\t * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n28\t * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n29\t */\n30\t\n31\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n32\t// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n33\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n34\t\n35\t// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n36\t// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n37\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n38\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n39\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n40\t\n41\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n42\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n43\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n44\t\n45\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n46\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n47\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n48\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n49\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n50\t}\n51\t\n52\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n55\t    return 'very-stale';\n56\t}\n57\t\n58\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n59\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n60\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n61\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n62\t\n63\t    const card = document.createElement('div');\n64\t    card.className = 'topic-card';\n65\t    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n66\t\n67\t    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n68\t    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n69\t\n70\t    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n71\t\n72\t    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n73\t    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n74\t    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n75\t    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n76\t    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n77\t\n78\t    let html = `\n79\t        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n80\t            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n81\t            <div>\n82\t                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${topic.type || 'adhoc'}</span>\n83\t                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n84\t            </div>\n85\t        </div>\n86\t    `;\n87\t\n88\t    // Add latest result if available\n89\t    if (latestResult) {\n90\t        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n91\t        html += `\n92\t            <div class=\"result-content\">\n93\t                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n94\t                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n95\t                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${urgencyClass}</span>\n96\t                </div>\n97\t        `;\n98\t\n99\t        if (latestResult.data) {\n100\t            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n101\t        }\n102\t\n103\t        html += `</div>`;\n104\t    }\n105\t\n106\t    // Add staleness indicator\n107\t    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n108\t    html += `\n109\t        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n110\t            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n111\t            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n112\t        </div>\n113\t    `;\n114\t\n115\t    card.innerHTML = html;\n116\t    return card;\n117\t}\n118\t\n119\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n120\t// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n121\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n122\t\n123\tfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n124\t    const div = document.createElement('div');\n125\t    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n126\t    return div.innerHTML;\n127\t}\n128\t\n129\t/**\n130\t * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n131\t *\n132\t * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n133\t * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n134\t * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n135\t * inserted verbatim as text.\n136\t */\n137\tfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n138\t    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n139\t    if (className) node.className = className;\n140\t    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n141\t        if (c == null) return;\n142\t        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n143\t            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n144\t        } else {\n145\t            node.appendChild(c);\n146\t        }\n147\t    });\n148\t    return node;\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\n152\tfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n153\t    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n154\t    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n155\t    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n156\t    const rem = s % 60;\n157\t    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n161\t// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n162\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n163\t\n164\t// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n165\t// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\n166\tvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n167\t    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n168\t    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n169\t    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n170\t];\n171\t\n172\t// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n173\t// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n174\t// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n175\t// projects' supported intents\").\n176\tvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n177\t    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n178\t    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n179\t    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n180\t    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n181\t    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n182\t    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n183\t    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n184\t    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n185\t};\n186\t\n187\tfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n188\t    const out = [];\n189\t    const seen = {};\n190\t    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n191\t    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n192\t    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n193\t    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n194\t        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n195\t        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n196\t        return ad - bd;\n197\t    });\n198\t    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n199\t        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n200\t        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n201\t            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n202\t            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n203\t            if (!tmpl) return;\n204\t            seen[intent] = true;\n205\t            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n206\t        });\n207\t    });\n208\t    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n209\t}\n210\t\n211\t// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n212\t// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\n213\tvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n214\t\n215\t/**\n216\t * Build the first-run welcome card.\n217\t *\n218\t * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n219\t * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n220\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n221\t *\n222\t * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n223\t * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n224\t * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n225\t */\n226\tfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n227\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n228\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n229\t\n230\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n231\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n232\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n233\t    ]));\n234\t\n235\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n236\t        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n237\t    ]));\n238\t\n239\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n240\t    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n241\t    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n242\t        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n243\t        const desc = p.description || '';\n244\t        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n245\t        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n246\t        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n247\t        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n248\t        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n249\t        list.appendChild(li);\n250\t    });\n251\t    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n252\t        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n253\t    }\n254\t    card.appendChild(list);\n255\t\n256\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n257\t    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n258\t    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n259\t        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n260\t    });\n261\t    card.appendChild(examples);\n262\t\n263\t    return card;\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n267\t// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n268\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n269\t\n270\t/**\n271\t * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n272\t *\n273\t * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n274\t * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n275\t * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n276\t * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n277\t * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n278\t * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n279\t *\n280\t * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n281\t * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n282\t * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n283\t * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n284\t *\n285\t * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n286\t * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n287\t * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n288\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n289\t */\n290\tfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n291\t    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n292\t        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n293\t    }\n294\t    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n298\t// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n299\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n300\t\n301\t/**\n302\t * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n303\t *\n304\t * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n305\t * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n306\t * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n307\t * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n308\t *\n309\t * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n310\t * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n311\t * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n312\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n313\t */\n314\tfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n315\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId);\n316\t    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n317\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n318\t    card.querySelector('.pending-title').textContent = 'Sending…';\n319\t    return card;\n320\t}\n321\t\n322\t/**\n323\t * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n324\t * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n325\t *\n326\t * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n327\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n328\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n329\t * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n330\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n331\t */\n332\tfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n333\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId);\n334\t    card.classList.add('thread');\n335\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n336\t    card.querySelector('.pending-title').textContent = 'Working on it…';\n337\t    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n338\t    return card;\n339\t}\n340\t\n341\t/**\n342\t * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n343\t * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n344\t * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n345\t * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n346\t * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n347\t * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n348\t * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n349\t *\n350\t * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n351\t * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n352\t * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n353\t * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n354\t *\n355\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n356\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n357\t * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n358\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n359\t */\n360\tfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n361\t    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n362\t        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n363\t    });\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\n367\tfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n368\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n369\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n370\t    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n371\t    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n372\t\n373\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n374\t        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n375\t        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', ['Pending…'])\n376\t    ]));\n377\t\n378\t    if (utterance) {\n379\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n380\t    }\n381\t\n382\t    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n383\t    progress.style.display = 'none';\n384\t    card.appendChild(progress);\n385\t\n386\t    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n387\t        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n388\t    ]);\n389\t    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n390\t\n391\t    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n392\t    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n393\t    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n394\t    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n395\t    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n396\t    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n397\t    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n398\t\n399\t    return card;\n400\t}\n401\t\n402\tfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n403\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n404\t    if (!node || !progress) return;\n405\t    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n406\t    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n407\t    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n408\t    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n409\t    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n410\t    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n411\t}\n412\t\n413\t/**\n414\t * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n415\t * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n416\t * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n417\t */\n418\tfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n419\t    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n420\t    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n421\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n422\t    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n423\t    return elapsedMs;\n424\t}\n425\t\n426\t/**\n427\t * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n428\t * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n429\t * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n430\t */\n431\tfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n432\t    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n433\t    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n434\t    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n435\t    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n436\t    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n437\t    return aged;\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n441\t// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n442\t//     from SSE error events\n443\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n444\t\n445\t// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n446\t// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n447\t// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\n448\tvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n449\t    router_unavailable: {\n450\t        title: 'Router unavailable',\n451\t        icon: '🔌',\n452\t        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n453\t    },\n454\t    all_sources_failed: {\n455\t        title: 'No data available',\n456\t        icon: '📭',\n457\t        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n458\t    },\n459\t    synthesis_failed: {\n460\t        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n461\t        icon: '⚠️',\n462\t        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n463\t    },\n464\t    malformed_router_output: {\n465\t        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n466\t        icon: '🧩',\n467\t        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n468\t    },\n469\t    no_match: {\n470\t        title: 'No matching project',\n471\t        icon: '❓',\n472\t        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n473\t    }\n474\t};\n475\t\n476\tfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n477\t    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n478\t    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n479\t    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n480\t}\n481\t\n482\t/**\n483\t * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n484\t *\n485\t * @param {object} data  {error_type, utterance, detail, sources, data, intent_id, pending_id}\n486\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n487\t *\n488\t * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n489\t * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n490\t */\n491\tfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n492\t    data = data || {};\n493\t    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n494\t    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n495\t\n496\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n497\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n498\t    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n499\t    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n500\t    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n501\t\n502\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n503\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n504\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n505\t    ]));\n506\t\n507\t    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n508\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n509\t\n510\t    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n511\t    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n512\t    if (data.utterance) {\n513\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n514\t            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n515\t            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n516\t        ]));\n517\t    }\n518\t\n519\t    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n520\t    const sources = data.sources;\n521\t    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n522\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n523\t        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n524\t            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n525\t            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n526\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n527\t                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n528\t                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n529\t            ]));\n530\t        });\n531\t        card.appendChild(ul);\n532\t    }\n533\t\n534\t    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n535\t    if (data.data != null) {\n536\t        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n537\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n538\t        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n539\t    }\n540\t\n541\t    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n542\t    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n543\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n544\t        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n545\t            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n546\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n547\t        });\n548\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n549\t            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n550\t            ul\n551\t        ]));\n552\t    }\n553\t\n554\t    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n555\t    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n556\t        ? 'Edit & resend'\n557\t        : 'Retry';\n558\t    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n559\t\n560\t    return card;\n561\t}\n562\t\n563\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n564\t// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n565\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n566\t\n567\t/**\n568\t * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n569\t * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n570\t * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n571\t * rendering escaping contract.\n572\t *\n573\t * @param {object} result  {summary, data, urgency}\n574\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n575\t */\n576\tfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n577\t    result = result || {};\n578\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n579\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n580\t\n581\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n582\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n583\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n584\t    ]));\n585\t\n586\t    if (result.summary) {\n587\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n588\t    }\n589\t\n590\t    const data = result.data;\n591\t    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n592\t    if (rows.length) {\n593\t        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n594\t        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n595\t            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n596\t            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n597\t        });\n598\t        card.appendChild(grid);\n599\t    }\n600\t\n601\t    if (result.urgency) {\n602\t        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n603\t    }\n604\t\n605\t    return card;\n606\t}\n607\t\n608\t// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\n609\tfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n610\t    if (data == null) return [];\n611\t    let obj = data;\n612\t    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n613\t        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n614\t    }\n615\t    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n616\t        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n617\t    }\n618\t    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n619\t        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n620\t    }\n621\t    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n622\t}\n623\t\n624\tfunction _stringify(v) {\n625\t    if (v == null) return '';\n626\t    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n627\t    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n628\t    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n629\t}\n630\t\n631\t// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n632\t// function declarations). In Node, expose them for the headless DOM tests.\n633\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n634\t    module.exports = {\n635\t        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n636\t
