* ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n *\n * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n *\n * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n * escaping contract.\n *\n * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n */\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\nvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Existing topic-card render helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n}\n\nfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n    return 'very-stale';\n}\n\nfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n    const topic = cardData.topic;\n    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n\n    const card = document.createElement('div');\n    card.className = 'topic-card';\n    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n\n    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n\n    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n\n    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n\n    let html = `\n        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n            <div>\n                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${topic.type || 'adhoc'}</span>\n                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    // Add latest result if available\n    if (latestResult) {\n        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n        html += `\n            <div class=\"result-content\">\n                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${urgencyClass}</span>\n                </div>\n        `;\n\n        if (latestResult.data) {\n            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n        }\n\n        html += `</div>`;\n    }\n\n    // Add staleness indicator\n    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n    html += `\n        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    card.innerHTML = html;\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n    const div = document.createElement('div');\n    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n    return div.innerHTML;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n *\n * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n * inserted verbatim as text.\n */\nfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n    if (className) node.className = className;\n    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n        if (c == null) return;\n        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n        } else {\n            node.appendChild(c);\n        }\n    });\n    return node;\n}\n\n// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\nfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n    const rem = s % 60;\n    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\nvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n];\n\n// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n// projects' supported intents\").\nvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n};\n\nfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n    const out = [];\n    const seen = {};\n    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n        return ad - bd;\n    });\n    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n            if (!tmpl) return;\n            seen[intent] = true;\n            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n        });\n    });\n    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n}\n\n// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\nvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n\n/**\n * Build the first-run welcome card.\n *\n * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n *\n * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n */\nfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n        const desc = p.description || '';\n        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n        list.appendChild(li);\n    });\n    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n    }\n    card.appendChild(list);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n    });\n    card.appendChild(examples);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n *\n * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n *\n * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n *\n * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n */\nfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n    }\n    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n *\n * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId);\n    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n    card.querySelector('.pending-title').textContent = 'Sending…';\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n *\n * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId);\n    card.classList.add('thread');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n    card.querySelector('.pending-title').textContent = 'Working on it…';\n    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n *\n * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n */\nfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n    });\n}\n\n// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\nfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', ['Pending…'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n    }\n\n    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n    progress.style.display = 'none';\n    card.appendChild(progress);\n\n    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n    ]);\n    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n\n    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\nfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n    if (!node || !progress) return;\n    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n}\n\n/**\n * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n */\nfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n    return elapsedMs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n */\nfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n    return aged;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n//     from SSE error events\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\nvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n    router_unavailable: {\n        title: 'Router unavailable',\n        icon: '🔌',\n        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n    },\n    all_sources_failed: {\n        title: 'No data available',\n        icon: '📭',\n        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n    },\n    synthesis_failed: {\n        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n        icon: '⚠️',\n        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n    },\n    malformed_router_output: {\n        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n        icon: '🧩',\n        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n    },\n    no_match: {\n        title: 'No matching project',\n        icon: '❓',\n        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n    }\n};\n\nfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n *\n * @param {object} data  {error_type, utterance, detail, sources, data, intent_id, pending_id}\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n *\n * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n */\nfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n    ]));\n\n    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n\n    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n    if (data.utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n    const sources = data.sources;\n    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n            ]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(ul);\n    }\n\n    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n    if (data.data != null) {\n        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n    }\n\n    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n            ul\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n        ? 'Edit & resend'\n        : 'Retry';\n    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n * rendering escaping contract.\n *\n * @param {object} result  {summary, data, urgency}\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n */\nfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n    result = result || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (result.summary) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n    }\n\n    const data = result.data;\n    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n    if (rows.length) {\n        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(grid);\n    }\n\n    if (result.urgency) {\n        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n    }\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\nfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n    if (data == null) return [];\n    let obj = data;\n    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n    }\n    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n    }\n    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n}\n\nfunction _stringify(v) {\n    if (v == null) return '';\n    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n}\n\n// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n// function declarations). In Node, expose them for the headless DOM tests.\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n    module.exports = {\n        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n        escapeHtml: escapeHtml,\n        formatStaleness: formatStaleness,\n        getStalenessLevel: getStalenessLevel,\n        // Built-in card families + helpers\n        el: el,\n        formatElapsed: formatElapsed,\n        createWelcomeCard: createWelcomeCard,\n        buildContainerChildren: buildContainerChildren,\n        createPendingPlaceholderCard: createPendingPlaceholderCard,\n        createPendingThreadCard: createPendingThreadCard,\n        splitPlaceholderToThreads: splitPlaceholderToThreads,\n        tickPendingElapsed: tickPendingElapsed,\n        applyAgedTreatment: applyAgedTreatment,\n        setPendingProgress: _setProgress,\n        createErrorCard: createErrorCard,\n        createFallbackCard: createFallbackCard,\n        normalizeErrorVariant: _normalizeVariant,\n        deriveExamples: _deriveExamples,\n        PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS: PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS,\n    };\n}\n","numLines":657,"startLine":1,"totalLines":657}}}

Bead adc-1l8w; ``--container`` mode (bead adc-4nd25).\n *\n * canvas.js is written so its render helpers (createTopicCard et al.) can be\n * exercised without a browser — its module docstring promises exactly this\n * file:\n *\n *     \"tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js loads this exact file under a minimal\n *      DOM shim and asserts the rendered HTML, with no browser or network\n *      required.\"\n *\n * This is that file. It provides a minimal DOM shim that implements precisely\n * the surface the render helpers touch — document.createElement,\n * document.createTextNode, appendChild, className, classList.add, dataset,\n * textContent (→ escaped innerHTML), and innerHTML get/set — then loads the\n * actual src/canvas/canvas.js and renders the card dicts the canvas really\n * receives from GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics.\n *\n * Two modes:\n *\n *   1. DEFAULT (bead adc-1l8w) — render an array of card dicts through\n *      createTopicCard(), the function loadTopics() calls per card. Drives the\n *      escaping contract for the topic-card family.\n *\n *   2. ``--container`` (bead adc-4nd25) — render the shared container-decision\n *      core buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description), which is what\n *      loadTopics() actually calls: on a fresh session with ZERO cards it\n *      returns the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project\n *      list — no DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it\n *      returns topic cards only (dropping the welcome card, never alongside\n *      real cards). This is the headlessly-testable contract the bead's\n *      acceptance criteria name: \"welcome card renders from a zero-card\n *      session and is dropped on first result.\"\n *\n * The Python contract tests (tests/test_canvas_render.py and\n * tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py) feed it JSON and assert the rendered\n * outerHTML, so a regression in the render contract is caught headlessly, in\n * CI, with no browser.\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node canvas_dom_runner.js '<json array of card dicts>'\n *   echo '<json array>' | node canvas_dom_runner.js        # stdin fallback\n *   node canvas_dom_runner.js --container '{\"cards\":[...],\"projects\":[...],\"description\":\"...\"}'\n *   echo '{...}' | node canvas_dom_runner.js --container   # stdin fallback\n *\n * Stdout: a JSON array of { outerHTML, className, dataset } per rendered node.\n */\n\n\"use strict\";\n\nconst fs = require(\"fs\");\nconst path = require(\"path\");\n\n// --- minimal DOM shim -------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    // Matches the browser: textContent→innerHTML escapes &, <, > (not quotes).\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\n\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// A text node (document.createTextNode). Its serialized form is the escaped\n// text — the escaping contract's literal form, since el()/createTextNode never\n// splice raw markup.\nclass ShimText {\n    constructor(text) {\n        this._text = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n        this.nodeType = 3;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._text = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._text;\n    }\n\n    get outerHTML() {\n        return escapeText(this._text);\n    }\n\n    get innerHTML() {\n        return escapeText(this._text);\n    }\n}\n\nclass ShimNode {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n        this._children = []; // built via appendChild (el()/createWelcomeCard path)\n        this.style = {}; // present so style.* assignments are no-ops, not crashes\n    }\n\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n    }\n\n    get className() {\n        return this._classes.join(\" \");\n    }\n\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) {\n                if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n            },\n            contains(c) {\n                return self._classes.includes(c);\n            },\n            toggle(c, force) {\n                const on = force === undefined ? !self._classes.includes(c) : !!force;\n                if (on && !self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n                if (!on) self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c);\n                return on;\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    get dataset() {\n        return this._dataset;\n    }\n\n    appendChild(node) {\n        this._children.push(node);\n        return node;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        // Setting textContent replaces all children with a single text node —\n        // mirrors the browser, and keeps escapeHtml()'s \"set textContent then\n        // read innerHTML\" pattern producing escaped text.\n        this._children = [];\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._textContent;\n    }\n\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._children = []; // innerHTML= replaces children, browser-faithful\n    }\n\n    get innerHTML() {\n        // Children built via appendChild serialize recursively; a directly-set\n        // innerHTML string (the createTopicCard path) is used otherwise.\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => c.outerHTML).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        // dataset.topicId → data-topic-id (camelCase → kebab-case w/ data- prefix).\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n            out.push(`${name}=\"${escapeAttr(this._dataset[key])}\"`);\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${this.innerHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n}\n\n// canvas.js references `document` as a free variable inside its functions, so\n// it must resolve via the global scope at call time.\nglobal.document = {\n    createElement(tag) {\n        return new ShimNode(tag);\n    },\n    createTextNode(text) {\n        return new ShimText(text);\n    },\n};\n\n// --- load the REAL canvas.js ------------------------------------------------\n\nconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\nconst canvas = require(canvasPath); // { createTopicCard, escapeHtml, formatStaleness, getStalenessLevel }\n\n// --- render driver ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Read the JSON payload for the active mode. argvPos is the index of the\n// positional JSON arg (after any --mode flag); stdin is the pipe-friendly\n// fallback. Returns the parsed value.\nfunction readPayload(argvPos) {\n    if (process.argv[argvPos] !== undefined && process.argv[argvPos] !== \"\") {\n        return JSON.parse(process.argv[argvPos]);\n    }\n    const raw = fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\");\n    return JSON.parse(raw);\n}\n\nfunction serialize(node) {\n    return {\n        outerHTML: node.outerHTML,\n        className: node.className,\n        dataset: { ...node.dataset },\n    };\n}\n\n// DEFAULT mode: render an array of card dicts through createTopicCard() — the\n// function loadTopics() calls per card after a reload.\nfunction runCardsMode() {\n    const cards = readPayload(2);\n    if (!Array.isArray(cards)) {\n        throw new Error(\"expected a JSON array of card dicts\");\n    }\n    const rendered = cards.map((card) => serialize(canvas.createTopicCard(card)));\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n}\n\n// --container mode (bead adc-4nd25): render buildContainerChildren() — the\n// shared render-path core loadTopics() calls. Empty cards → the welcome card;\n// any card → topic cards only (welcome dropped). The input object mirrors what\n// loadTopics() builds: cards from GET /topics, projects from GET /registry,\n// and an optional one-line description.\nfunction runContainerMode() {\n    const payload = readPayload(3);\n    const cards = (payload && payload.cards) || [];\n    const projects = (payload && payload.projects) || [];\n    const description = payload && payload.description;\n    const nodes = canvas.buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description);\n    const rendered = nodes.map((node) => serialize(node));\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n}\n\nfunction main() {\n    if (process.argv[2] === \"--container\") {\n        runContainerMode();\n    } else {\n        runCardsMode();\n    }\n}\n\ntry {\n    main();\n} catch (err) {\n    process.stderr.write(`canvas_dom_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n    process.exit(1);\n}\n","numLines":261,"startLine":1,"totalLines":261}}}

The headlessly-testable core of that wiring is\n8\t``buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description)``: empty ``cards`` → the\n9\twelcome card; any real card → topic cards only (welcome dropped, never shown\n10\talongside a real card).\n11\t\n12\tThis suite drives that exact function through the Node DOM runner\n13\t(``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js --container``) under a minimal DOM shim — no\n14\tbrowser, no live server, no DB — and asserts the bead's acceptance criteria:\n15\t\n16\t* a zero-card (fresh) session renders the welcome card, its registered-project\n17\t  list, and **>=2** example utterances;\n18\t* the welcome card is **replaced, not duplicated**, the moment the first real\n19\t  result lands (a non-empty card set yields topic cards only);\n20\t* every interpolated value is a text node — markup in a registry slug or\n21\t  description surfaces as visible text, never as injected HTML (the escaping\n22\t  contract, bead adc-3ixa).\n23\t\n24\tIt is the Python companion the runner's own docstring names\n25\t(\"tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py\"), mirroring how\n26\t``tests/test_canvas_dom_verify.py`` covers the topic-card family.\n27\t\"\"\"\n28\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n29\t\n30\tfrom html.parser import HTMLParser\n31\t\n32\timport pytest\n33\t\n34\tfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import node_available, render_container\n35\t\n36\tpytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n37\t    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\"\n38\t)\n39\t\n40\t\n41\t# --- minimal selector-style DOM query over stdlib html.parser ------------------\n42\t# BeautifulSoup/lxml are intentionally not a dependency; createWelcomeCard()\n43\t# emits simple, well-formed HTML we parse with the standard library. This\n44\t# mirrors the query style in tests/test_canvas_dom_verify.py.\n45\t\n46\t\n47\tclass _Node:\n48\t    __slots__ = (\"tag\", \"attrs\", \"classes\", \"children\", \"text_parts\")\n49\t\n50\t    def __init__(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:\n51\t        self.tag = tag\n52\t        self.attrs = {k: (v if v is not None else \"\") for k, v in attrs}\n53\t        cls = self.attrs.get(\"class\", \"\")\n54\t        self.classes = set(cls.split()) if cls else set()\n55\t        self.children: list[_Node] = []\n56\t        self.text_parts: list[str] = []\n57\t\n58\t    def _walk(self):\n59\t        for child in self.children:\n60\t            yield child\n61\t            yield from child._walk()\n62\t\n63\t    def find_all(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n64\t        out: list[_Node] = []\n65\t        for node in self._walk():\n66\t            if tag is not None and node.tag != tag:\n67\t                continue\n68\t            if class_ is not None and class_ not in node.classes:\n69\t                continue\n70\t            out.append(node)\n71\t        return out\n72\t\n73\t    def find(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None) -> \"_Node | None\":\n74\t        matches = self.find_all(tag=tag, class_=class_)\n75\t        return matches[0] if matches else None\n76\t\n77\t    def has_class(self, class_: str) -> bool:\n78\t        return class_ in self.classes\n79\t\n80\t    @property\n81\t    def text(self) -> str:\n82\t        parts = list(self.text_parts)\n83\t        for node in self._walk():\n84\t            parts.extend(node.text_parts)\n85\t        return \" \".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()\n86\t\n87\t\n88\t_VOID = {\"area\", \"base\", \"br\", \"col\", \"embed\", \"hr\", \"img\", \"input\",\n89\t         \"link\", \"meta\", \"param\", \"source\", \"track\", \"wbr\"}\n90\t\n91\t\n92\tclass _TreeBuilder(HTMLParser):\n93\t    def __init__(self) -> None:\n94\t        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=True)\n95\t        self.root = _Node(\"__root__\", [])\n96\t        self._stack: list[_Node] = [self.root]\n97\t\n98\t    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):\n99\t        node = _Node(tag, attrs)\n100\t        self._stack[-1].children.append(node)\n101\t        if tag not in _VOID:\n102\t            self._stack.append(node)\n103\t\n104\t    def handle_startendtag(self, tag, attrs):\n105\t        self._stack[-1].children.append(_Node(tag, attrs))\n106\t\n107\t    def handle_endtag(self, tag):\n108\t        for i in range(len(self._stack) - 1, 0, -1):\n109\t            if self._stack[i].tag == tag:\n110\t                del self._stack[i:]\n111\t                break\n112\t\n113\t    def handle_data(self, data):\n114\t        s = data.strip()\n115\t        if s:\n116\t            self._stack[-1].text_parts.append(s)\n117\t\n118\t\n119\tdef parse(node_out: dict) -> _Node:\n120\t    \"\"\"Parse one ``render_container`` output dict into a queryable tree; returns\n121\t    the rendered root element.\"\"\"\n122\t    tb = _TreeBuilder()\n123\t    tb.feed(node_out[\"outerHTML\"])\n124\t    tb.close()\n125\t    assert tb.root.children, \"rendered node had no root element\"\n126\t    return tb.root.children[0]\n127\t\n128\t\n129\tdef _project(slug: str, *, description: str = \"\", intents: list[str] | None = None) render-contract test for the first-run welcome card (bead adc-4nd25).\n\nThe welcome card builder (``createWelcomeCard``) ships in the served frontend\nmodule ``src/canvas/canvas.js``; this bead WIRING job makes ``loadTopics()``\nactually render it on a fresh session (zero topics) instead of the bare\n\"No active topics\" empty-state. The headlessly-testable core of that wiring is\n``buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description)``: empty ``cards`` → the\nwelcome card; any real card → topic cards only (welcome dropped, never shown\nalongside a real card).\n\nThis suite drives that exact function through the Node DOM runner\n(``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js --container``) under a minimal DOM shim — no\nbrowser, no live server, no DB — and asserts the bead's acceptance criteria:\n\n* a zero-card (fresh) session renders the welcome card, its registered-project\n  list, and **>=2** example utterances;\n* the welcome card is **replaced, not duplicated**, the moment the first real\n  result lands (a non-empty card set yields topic cards only);\n* every interpolated value is a text node — markup in a registry slug or\n  description surfaces as visible text, never as injected HTML (the escaping\n  contract, bead adc-3ixa).\n\nIt is the Python companion the runner's own docstring names\n(\"tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py\"), mirroring how\n``tests/test_canvas_dom_verify.py`` covers the topic-card family.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nfrom html.parser import HTMLParser\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import node_available, render_container\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\"\n)\n\n\n# --- minimal selector-style DOM query over stdlib html.parser ------------------\n# BeautifulSoup/lxml are intentionally not a dependency; createWelcomeCard()\n# emits simple, well-formed HTML we parse with the standard library. This\n# mirrors the query style in tests/test_canvas_dom_verify.py.\n\n\nclass _Node:\n    __slots__ = (\"tag\", \"attrs\", \"classes\", \"children\", \"text_parts\")\n\n    def __init__(self, tag: str, attrs: list[tuple[str, str | None]]) -> None:\n        self.tag = tag\n        self.attrs = {k: (v if v is not None else \"\") for k, v in attrs}\n        cls = self.attrs.get(\"class\", \"\")\n        self.classes = set(cls.split()) if cls else set()\n        self.children: list[_Node] = []\n        self.text_parts: list[str] = []\n\n    def _walk(self):\n        for child in self.children:\n            yield child\n            yield from child._walk()\n\n    def find_all(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None) -> list[\"_Node\"]:\n        out: list[_Node] = []\n        for node in self._walk():\n            if tag is not None and node.tag != tag:\n                continue\n            if class_ is not None and class_ not in node.classes:\n                continue\n            out.append(node)\n        return out\n\n    def find(self, *, tag: str | None = None, class_: str | None = None) -> \"_Node | None\":\n        matches = self.find_all(tag=tag, class_=class_)\n        return matches[0] if matches else None\n\n    def has_class(self, class_: str) -> bool:\n        return class_ in self.classes\n\n    @property\n    def text(self) -> str:\n        parts = list(self.text_parts)\n        for node in self._walk():\n            parts.extend(node.text_parts)\n        return \" \".join(p for p in parts if p).strip()\n\n\n_VOID = {\"area\", \"base\", \"br\", \"col\", \"embed\", \"hr\", \"img\", \"input\",\n         \"link\", \"meta\", \"param\", \"source\", \"track\", \"wbr\"}\n\n\nclass _TreeBuilder(HTMLParser):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__(convert_charrefs=True)\n        self.root = _Node(\"__root__\", [])\n        self._stack: list[_Node] = [self.root]\n\n    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):\n        node = _Node(tag, attrs)\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(node)\n        if tag not in _VOID:\n            self._stack.append(node)\n\n    def handle_startendtag(self, tag, attrs):\n        self._stack[-1].children.append(_Node(tag, attrs))\n\n    def handle_endtag(self, tag):\n        for i in range(len(self._stack) - 1, 0, -1):\n            if self._stack[i].tag == tag:\n                del self._stack[i:]\n                break\n\n    def handle_data(self, data):\n        s = data.strip()\n        if s:\n            self._stack[-1].text_parts.append(s)\n\n\ndef parse(node_out: dict) -> _Node:\n    \"\"\"Parse one ``render_container`` output dict into a queryable tree; returns\n    the rendered root element.\"\"\"\n    tb = _TreeBuilder()\n    tb.feed(node_out[\"outerHTML\"])\n    tb.close()\n    assert tb.root.children, \"rendered node had no root element\"\n    return tb.root.children[0]\n\n\ndef _project(slug: str, *, description: str = \"\", intents: list[str] | None = None) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A registry entry in the shape GET /api/v1/registry returns under .projects.\"\"\"\n    return {\"slug\": slug, \"description\": description, \"intent_support\": intents or []}\n\n\n# Two described projects with supported intents, so examples are derived from\n# real registry entries (not the default fallback set).\n_PROJECTS = [\n parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n            }\n            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n            }\n            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n            }\n\n            if (parts.length > 0) {\n                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        session_id: sessionId,\n                        surface_type: surfaceType\n                    })\n                });\n\n                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n        let welcomeProjects = null;\n\n        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n                if (resp.ok) {\n                    const data = await resp.json();\n                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n                    return welcomeProjects;\n                }\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n            }\n            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n            welcomeProjects = [];\n            return welcomeProjects;\n        }\n\n        async function loadTopics() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n                if (!response.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n                const cards = data.cards || [];\n\n                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n                    container.appendChild(node);\n                });\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        function connectSSE() {\n            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n\n            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n\n            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n                loadTopics();\n            };\n\n            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n            };\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('New result:', result);\n                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n                loadTopics();\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Server disconnect:', data);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n                eventSource.close();\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Heartbeat to keep surface alive\n        setInterval(() => {\n            if (surfaceId) {\n                fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        surface_id: surfaceId,\n                        session_id: sessionId\n                    })\n                }).catch(console.error);\n            }\n        }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds\n\n        // ── Input bar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        const textarea = document.getElementById('inputTextarea');\n        const btnSend  = document.getElementById('btnSend');\n        const btnMic   = document.getElementById('btnMic');\n\n        // Auto-resize textarea\n        textarea.addEventListener('input', () => {\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n            btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n        });\n\n        // Send on Enter (Shift+Enter = newline)\n        textarea.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {\n                e.preventDefault();\n                if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n            }\n        });\n\n        btnSend.addEventListener('click', () => {\n            if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n        });\n\n        async function dispatch(utterance) {\n            if (!utterance) return;\n            textarea.value = '';\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            btnSend.disabled = true;\n\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/dispatch', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId }),\n                });\n                if (!resp.ok) {\n                    const err = await resp.text();\n                    console.error('Dispatch error:', err);\n                }\n            } catch (err) {\n                console.error('Dispatch failed:', err);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Web Speech API mic\n        let recognition = null;\n\n        if ('webkitSpeechRecognition' in window || 'SpeechRecognition' in window) {\n            const SpeechRecognition = window.SpeechRecognition || window.webkitSpeechRecognition;\n            recognition = new SpeechRecognition();\n            recognition.continuous = false;\n            recognition.interimResults = true;\n            recognition.lang = 'en-US';\n\n            let finalTranscript = '';\n\n            recognition.onstart = () => {\n                btnMic.classList.add('recording');\n                finalTranscript = textarea.value;\n            };\n\n            recognition.onresult = (e) => {\n                let interim = '';\n                for (let i = e.resultIndex; i < e.results.length; i++) {\n                    if (e.results[i].isFinal) {\n                        finalTranscript += e.results[i][0].transcript;\n                    } else {\n                        interim += e.results[i][0].transcript;\n                    }\n                }\n                textarea.value = finalTranscript + interim;\n                textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n                textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n                btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n            };\n\n            recognition.onend = () => {\n                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n                if (finalTranscript.trim()) dispatch(finalTranscript.trim());\n            };\n\n            recognition.onerror = (e) => {\n                console.error('Speech recognition error:', e.error);\n                btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n            };\n\n            btnMic.addEventListener('click', () => {\n                if (btnMic.classList.contains('recording')) {\n                    recognition.stop();\n                } else {\n                    recognition.start();\n                }\n            });\n        } else {\n            // Fallback: MediaRecorder + whisper-stt for browsers without Web Speech API\n            let mediaRecorder = null;\n            let audioChunks = [];\n\n            async function startRecording() {\n                try {\n                    const stream = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true });\n                    mediaRecorder = new MediaRecorder(stream);\n                    audioChunks = [];\n\n                    mediaRecorder.ondataavailable = (event) => {\n                        if (event.data.size > 0) {\n                            audioChunks.push(event.data);\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    mediaRecorder.onstop = async () => {\n                        btnMic.classList.remove('recording');\n                        btnMic.innerHTML = '🎤';\n\n                        // Create audio blob and convert to base64\n                        const audioBlob = new Blob(audioChunks, { type: 'audio/webm' });\n                        const reader = new FileReader();\n                        reader.onloadend = async () => {\n                            const base64Audio = reader.result.split(',')[1];\n\n                            try {\n                                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/stt', {\n                                    method: 'POST',\n                                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                                        audio_data: base64Audio,\n                                        format: 'webm'\n                                    })\n                                });\n\n                                if (resp.ok) {\n                                    const data = await resp.json();\n                                    if (data.text) {\n                                        textarea.value = data.text;\n                                        textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n                                        textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n                                        btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n                                        // Auto-dispatch on successful transcription\n                                        dispatch(data.text.trim());\n                                    }\n                                } else {\n                                    const err = await resp.json();\n                                    console.error('STT error:', err);\n                                    btnMic.title = `Transcription failed: ${err.error || 'Unknown error'}`;\n                                    setTimeout(() => {\n                                        btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n                                    }, 3000);\n                                }\n                            } catch (err) {\n                                console.error('STT request failed:', err);\n                                btnMic.title = 'STT service unavailable';\n                                setTimeout(() => {\n                                    btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n                                }, 3000);\n                            }\n                        };\n                        reader.readAsDataURL(audioBlob);\n\n                        // Stop all tracks to release microphone\n                        stream.getTracks().forEach(track => track.stop());\n                    };\n\n                    mediaRecorder.start();\n                    btnMic.classList.add('recording');\n                    btnMic.innerHTML = '⏹';\n                } catch (err) {\n                    console.error('MediaRecorder access failed:', err);\n                    btnMic.title = 'Microphone access denied';\n                    btnMic.style.opacity = '0.4';\n                    btnMic.disabled = true;\n                }\n            }\n\n            function stopRecording() {\n                if (mediaRecorder && mediaRecorder.state === 'recording') {\n                    mediaRecorder.stop();\n                }\n            }\n\n            btnMic.title = 'Click to record (whisper-stt fallback)';\n            btnMic.disabled = false;\n            btnMic.style.opacity = '1';\n\n            btnMic.addEventListener('click', () => {\n                if (btnMic.classList.contains('recording')) {\n                    stopRecording();\n                } else {\n                    startRecording();\n                }\n            });\n        }\n\n        // ── Initialize ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        // Initialize\n        async function init() {\n            try {\n                // Show version\n                const meta = await fetch('/openapi.json').then(r => r.json()).catch(() => null);\n                if (meta && meta.info && meta.info.version) {\n                    document.getElementById('versionBadge').textContent = 'v' + meta.info.version;\n                }\n\n                await registerSurface();\n                connectSSE();\n            } catch (error) {\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Failed to connect');\n                console.error('Initialization error:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        init();\n     // Build a stable CSS selector for a given element\n        function buildSelector(el) {\n            if (!el || el === document.body) return 'body';\n            var parts = [];\n            var cur = el;\n            while (cur && cur !== document.body && cur !== document.documentElement) {\n                if (cur.id) { parts.unshift('#' + cur.id); break; }\n                var tag = cur.tagName.toLowerCase();\n                var classes = Array.from(cur.classList)\n                    .filter(function (c) { return c.length > 1; })\n                    .slice(0, 2);\n                var part = tag + (classes.length ? '.' + classes.join('.') : '');\n                // add nth-of-type when siblings share the same tag\n                if (cur.parentElement) {\n                    var sibs = Array.from(cur.parentElement.children)\n                        .filter(function (s) { return s.tagName === cur.tagName; });\n                    if (sibs.length > 1) part += ':nth-of-type(' + (sibs.indexOf(cur) + 1) + ')';\n                }\n                parts.unshift(part);\n                cur = cur.parentElement;\n            }\n            return parts.join(' > ');\n        }\n\n        // Highlight box\n        var hlBox = document.createElement('div');\n        hlBox.style.cssText = [\n            'position:fixed', 'pointer-events:none', 'z-index:9998',\n document.body.appendChild(hlBox);\n\n        function moveHighlight(el) {\n            if (!el || el === document.body) { hlBox.style.display = 'none'; return; }\n            var r = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n            hlBox.style.cssText = hlBox.style.cssText; // keep existing\n            hlBox.style.display = 'block';\n            hlBox.style.left  = (r.left   - 2) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.top   = (r.top    - 2) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.width = (r.width  + 4) + 'px';\n            hlBox.style.height= (r.height + 4) + 'px';\n        }\n\n        //   = buildSelector(selectedEl);\n            var elHtml  = selectedEl.outerHTML.slice(0, 2000);\n            var sendBtn = document.getElementById('atn-send');\n            sendBtn.textContent =
